mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer demand and residential investment, which reduces near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces negative forward equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline MoM
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show no statistically significant difference in forward returns compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which improves short-term earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when applied outside early-cycle expansion phases, or forward returns are negative during rising INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates expanding labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth consistently exceeds real GDP growth, it indicates rising capacity utilization and improving corporate profit margins, which translates to positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows; forward returns are not significantly positive when supply growth leads demand growth.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflationary environment characterized by elevated inflation and decelerating retail sales, equity markets price in simultaneous margin compression and demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when applied to non-stagflationary periods, or forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances.
